OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion:
As a middle-aged man with my own history, I believe the best way to handle conversations on COVID vaccines is through patience and clear explanation. My personality makes me someone who values direct communication without being too pushy, so when people bring up doubts or misinformation online, it’s important to respond calmly. Having been married for many years and raised four kids myself, I know that building trust takes time—especially with topics as sensitive as public health.

I also remember teaching in New York City during the pandemic; there were countless discussions about school policies and vaccines among parents and colleagues. Because of my moderate agreeableness score from the Big Five personality test, I found it easier to focus on finding common ground rather than winning arguments point by point. People needed reassurance that their children would be okay if they didn’t get vaccinated themselves.

Moreover, being somewhat self-employed as a school teacher allows me flexibility in addressing these issues whenever my time permits—whether during parent-teacher conferences or when reading comments online about the vaccines.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary:
1. My age (63) and life experiences shape an approach based on wisdom rather than radical change.
2. The moderate agreeableness from Big Five indicates a preference for collaborative dialogue over confrontation in discussions.
3. Teaching background provides practical experience with explaining complex topics to diverse audiences.
4. Self-employment offers freedom to engage thoughtfully without strict schedules.
